title: Boat Rental in Brighton: Tips, Costs and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Brighton offers excellent boat rental options for exploring the English Channel. Depart from Brighton Marina with prices starting from £295 for a half-day on a small motorboat. Whether you need a skippered charter or a self-drive vessel, BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators and a wide selection of boats.

Where to set off in Brighton

Brighton Marina is the primary departure point, offering full facilities including fuelling and parking. Nearby Shoreham Harbour provides an alternative for smaller vessels. For longer trips, you can head towards Beachy Head or the Isle of Wight. Renting a boat gives you access to the Seven Sisters cliffs and Eastbourne.

What is the best season for boat rental in Brighton?

The best season runs from May to September, with July and August being peak months. During this period, the southwesterly winds are generally moderate, making for comfortable cruising. Shoulder months like May and September offer quieter marinas and lower prices. Always check local forecasts before setting out.

For a calmer day on the water, aim for a morning departure when the sea breeze has not yet strengthened.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Brighton?

Prices vary from £295 for a half-day on a small motorboat to £1,200 for a full-day crewed yacht. For example, a shared sightseeing tour can start from £45 per person. To compare verified boat listings and find the best deal, yacht charter Brighton offers a wide range of options. The cost typically includes the boat, safety equipment, and sometimes a skipper.

  • Shared tour: from £45 per person for a 2-hour trip
  • Small motorboat half-day: from £295 (up to 6 guests)
  • Catamaran full-day: from £800 (up to 12 guests)
  • Luxury crewed yacht full-day: from £1,200 (up to 8 guests)

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat, Best for short coastal hops or watersports; ideal for up to 6 guests; from £295 half-day
  2. Sailboat, Perfect for a relaxed day sail; fits 4, 8 guests; from £500 full-day
  3. Catamaran, Stable and spacious for groups; up to 12 guests; from £800 full-day
  4. Luxury Motor Yacht (e.g. Princess F70), Ultimate comfort for a special occasion; up to 10 guests; from £1,500 full-day. For full specifications, refer to the Princess F70 listing.
  5. Rib, Fast and fun for sightseeing; up to 8 guests; from £350 half-day

    6. Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Brighton?

    In the UK, you do not need a licence for a private pleasure boat, but most rental operators require a valid RYA Powerboat Level 2 or equivalent. If you lack a licence, you can hire a skippered charter or take a taster session. Always confirm the specific requirements with the operator when booking.

    At a glance

    • Price band: £45, £1,500 per trip
    • Peak months: July, August
    • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
    • Typical capacity: 6, 12 guests
    • Currency: GBP

    Questions about boat rental in Brighton

    Can I rent a boat for a few hours in Brighton?

    Yes, many operators offer half-day (4-hour) rentals starting from £295 for a small motorboat.

    Are there guided tours on the water from Brighton?

    Yes, shared sightseeing tours from Brighton Marina are available from £45 per person, often visiting Beachy Head.

    Can I rent a boat without a captain?

    Yes, self-drive options are available if you hold a recognised boating certificate, such as RYA Powerboat Level 2.

    What is the best way to book a boat in Brighton?

    Use BoatHire24 to compare options and book securely online; the platform lists verified boats with transparent pricing.

    BoatHire24 makes it simple to find and rent a boat in Brighton for a day on the sea. Whether you are planning a family outing or a corporate event, the marketplace offers everything from ribs to luxury yachts. If you own a vessel, you can also list your boat and start earning.

    Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Brighton and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
